budget_wines.name: Alvaro Palacios Camins del Priorat
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely distributed through Spanish wine merchants and international importers. Available at specialist wine retailers in the UK, US, and EU. Winery contact via alvaropalacios.com.
budget_wines.description: Camins del Priorat is the entry tier from Alvaro Palacios, blending Garnacha and Carinena sourced from eight municipalities across the DOQ. Fruit-forward and accessible, it represents the accessible face of the estate without sacrificing the minerality of the licorella soils.
budget_wines.tip: Widely distributed in export markets. A multi-varietal blend of Garnacha, Carinena, and small parcels of Syrah and Merlot.

budget_wines.name: Mas Martinet Bru
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through specialist Spanish wine importers in the UK, US, and Netherlands. The Natural Wine Company (UK) lists it. Winery contact via masmartinet.com.
budget_wines.description: Bru is the entry-level bottling from Mas Martinet, combining Garnacha with Carinena and Syrah from organically farmed vines in Falset. Sara Perez's agroecological approach is reflected in the freshness and precision of the wine despite its approachable price.
budget_wines.tip: Mas Martinet Bru is the most accessible way to experience Sara Perez's biodynamic-leaning farming philosophy without the price of the estate's top single-parcel wines.

budget_wines.name: Buil & Gine Gine Gine
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through Buil & Gine's webshop at prioratshop.com and through Spanish wine importers across Europe and North America.
budget_wines.description: Gine Gine is an equal-parts Garnacha and Carinena blend from Buil & Gine, grown on licorella slate soils and crafted to show the essence of Priorat in an accessible format. Wild strawberry and cherry fruit lead with a characteristic earthy slate mineral note.
budget_wines.tip: Gine Gine 2019 won Best of Show Priorat at Mundus Vini. A consistent benchmark for under-EUR 25 Priorat.

budget_wines.name: Vinicola del Priorat Onix Classic
budget_wines.where_to_buy: One of the most widely distributed cooperative Priorat wines. Available at The Wine Feed (US), Vinello (UK), and Spanish supermarkets. Imported by Fine Estates From Spain.
budget_wines.description: Onix Classic from the Vinicola del Priorat cooperative is an equal-parts Carinena and Garnacha blend from 60-plus-year-old vines, hand-harvested from llicorella slate soils. The rare under-EUR 15 Priorat with genuine old-vine character: dark ruby, plum and earth on the nose, soft tannins and a clean finish. The cooperative entry to the DOQ.
budget_wines.tip: At under EUR 15 in Spain and around EUR 16 in northern Europe, Onix Classic is the most accessible Priorat from old vines on licorella. A genuine co-op wine from within the DOQ rather than the surrounding Montsant.

budget_wines.name: Vall Llach Embruix
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through Bottles Fine Wine and Corkery Wine in the US, and through Spanish wine specialists across Europe.
budget_wines.description: Embruix (meaning 'bewitching' in Catalan) is the entry wine from Vall Llach, blending multiple varieties from younger estate vines in Porrera. The label features a full moon, a nod to the estate's interest in biodynamic winemaking. Approachable Priorat character with red fruit and a mineral backbone.
budget_wines.tip: Embruix provides an entry point to Vall Llach's Porrera terroir at a fraction of the price of their village and single-parcel bottlings.

budget_wines.name: Cims de Porrera Solanes
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through MYSA Natural Wine, Parnes Wines, and specialist Spanish importers in the US and UK.
budget_wines.description: Cims de Porrera was created by Josep Lluis Perez to rescue century-old Carinena and Garnacha vines from abandonment in the village of Porrera. Solanes is the entry bottling from the estate, grown on steep 40 to 50 percent gradient licorella slopes. Old vines, organic farming, and the structured Porrera terroir in an accessible format.
budget_wines.tip: Cims de Porrera Solanes offers old-vine Carinena from Porrera at a price well below the estate's Vi de Vila bottlings. An under-recognised option from a historically important estate.

budget_wines.name: Marco Abella Mas Mallola
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through WoodWinters Wines (UK) and Spanish wine specialists. The winery operates from Porrera.
budget_wines.description: Mas Mallola is Marco Abella's entry-level Priorat from biodynamically farmed terraced vineyards in Porrera. The Marco family has roots in Porrera dating to 1497 according to Catalan census records, returned to their estate in 1898, and built their modern gravity-fed winery in 2001. Four separate terraced plots at altitudes from 200 to 700 metres produce concentrated but fresh Garnacha-Carinena blends.
budget_wines.tip: The Marco Abella estate is one of the few in Priorat with documented roots before the 15th century. Mas Mallola is the affordable entry to this historic licorella property.

budget_wines.name: Mas d'en Compte Porrera
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through Chapel Hill Wine Company and Woodland Hills Wine Company in the US.
budget_wines.description: Mas d'en Compte is a Sangenis family property in Porrera farming 60-year-old Carinena and 15-year-old Garnacha and Cabernet Sauvignon on deep, rocky schist soils across costers and terraces. The estate entry wine offers Porrera's characteristic structured profile from old-vine Carinena at an entry-level price point.
budget_wines.tip: Mas d'en Compte's wines are anchored in Porrera's Carinena heritage. The entry bottle is the most accessible way to experience this village's distinctive mineral structure.

budget_wines.name: Pasanau Ceps Nous
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through Spanish wine specialists and the winery direct. Cellar Pasanau is based in la Morera de Montsant in the higher-altitude northern sector of the DOQ.
budget_wines.description: Ceps Nous ('new vines' in Catalan) is Pasanau's entry-level Priorat, sourced from younger estate vines at higher-altitude plantings around la Morera de Montsant. The Pasanau family's sky-high plots benefit from cooler nights and longer hang time, adding freshness to the concentrated licorella character. Good value for accessible Priorat from an altitude-focused estate.
budget_wines.tip: Pasanau's higher-altitude la Morera de Montsant plots are among the coolest in the DOQ. Ceps Nous offers a lighter style than lower-valley Priorat at a comparable price.

verified.address_quoted: La Morera de Montsant, Priorat, Catalonia

budget_wines.name: Mas d'en Gil Coma Vella
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through Manicaretti Imports in the US and through Spanish wine specialists.
budget_wines.description: Mas d'en Gil is a family estate in Bellmunt del Priorat with 125 hectares of vineyards, olive groves, and Mediterranean woodland. The Rovira family introduced biodynamic practices from 2008 and maintains organic certification across the property. Coma Vella is the entry-level wine from the estate, offering organically farmed Bellmunt fruit at an approachable price.
budget_wines.tip: Mas d'en Gil holds organic certification across the 125-hectare estate (vineyards, olive groves, Mediterranean woodland), with biodynamic practices applied since 2008.
